passage.title: A Day of Decisions
passage.text: It was a busy Monday morning at the office. Rahul walked in and said to his manager, 'Let me handle the client presentation today.' The manager agreed. 'Let him do it,' she told the team. During the meeting, Rahul said, 'Let me give you an example of how our product works.' After the client left, the manager said to the team, 'Let us review what went well.' She also reminded everyone, 'Don't let small mistakes become big problems.' Later that evening, Rahul wrote in his diary: 'Let me always be prepared. Let go of the fear of failure. Don't let one bad day define the journey.' It was a day full of learning and growth.
passage.hindiTranslation: सोमवार की सुबह office में बहुत काम था। राहुल अंदर आया और अपने manager से बोला, 'मुझे आज client presentation handle करने दो।' Manager ने हाँ कर दिया। उसने team से कहा, 'उसे करने दो।' Meeting में राहुल ने कहा, 'मुझे आपको एक example देने दो कि हमारा product कैसे काम करता है।' Client के जाने के बाद manager ने team से कहा, 'हमें देखने दो कि क्या अच्छा हुआ।' उसने सबको याद दिलाया, 'छोटी गलतियों को बड़ी समस्या मत बनने दो।' शाम को राहुल ने अपनी diary में लिखा: 'मुझे हमेशा तैयार रहने दो। डर को जाने दो। एक बुरे दिन को पूरे सफर को define मत करने दो।' यह सीखने और बढ़ने का दिन था।

comprehensionQuestions.question: What did Rahul say to his manager at the start of the day?
options: Let me handle the client presentation today.
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Let me handle the client presentation today.
comprehensionQuestions.explanation: Directly stated in the passage: Rahul said 'Let me handle the client presentation today.'

comprehensionQuestions.question: What did the manager say to the team about Rahul?
comprehensionQuestions.explanation: The manager said to the team: 'Let him do it.'

comprehensionQuestions.question: How did Rahul explain the product during the meeting?
options: Let me give you an example of how our product works.
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Let me give you an example of how our product works.
comprehensionQuestions.explanation: Rahul said: 'Let me give you an example of how our product works.'

comprehensionQuestions.question: What did the manager say after the client left?
options: Don't let the client come back.
Let us review what went well.
Let me talk to the client.
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Let us review what went well.
comprehensionQuestions.explanation: The manager said: 'Let us review what went well.'

comprehensionQuestions.question: What did the manager remind the team?
options: Don't let small mistakes become big problems.
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Don't let small mistakes become big problems.
comprehensionQuestions.explanation: Manager reminded: 'Don't let small mistakes become big problems.'

comprehensionQuestions.question: What did Rahul write in his diary about fear?
options: Let go of the fear of failure.
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Let go of the fear of failure.
comprehensionQuestions.explanation: Rahul wrote: 'Let go of the fear of failure.'

comprehensionQuestions.question: Which 'Don't let' sentence did Rahul use in his diary?
options: Don't let one bad day define the journey.
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Don't let one bad day define the journey.
comprehensionQuestions.explanation: Rahul's diary entry: 'Don't let one bad day define the journey.'

comprehensionQuestions.question: What is the overall theme of this passage?
options: Using 'Let' for permission, decisions, and motivation
comprehensionQuestions.answer: Using 'Let' for permission, decisions, and motivation
comprehensionQuestions.explanation: The passage demonstrates Let me, Let him, Let us, Don't let — all covering permission and decisions.

comprehensionQuestions.question: Which sentence from the passage uses 'Let' as a self-directed statement?
options: Don't let small mistakes become problems.
comprehensionQuestions.explanation: 'Let me always be prepared' = self-directed motivational use of 'Let me'.

comprehensionQuestions.question: What type of 'Let' structure is 'Don't let small mistakes become big problems'?
comprehensionQuestions.explanation: 'Don't let' + object + base verb = negative form of Let.
